audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot tabernacle construction and inventory text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for bronze altar, basin from serving women mirrors, court, screen, and material inventory by Ithamar.

verses.restored: He made altar of burnt offering of acacia wood: five cubits its length and five cubits its width, square, and three cubits its height.
He made its horns on its four corners; its horns were from it, and he overlaid it with bronze.
He made all vessels of altar: pots, shovels, basins, forks, and firepans; all its vessels he made bronze.
He made for altar grating, network of bronze, under its ledge from below to its middle.
He cast four rings on four ends of bronze grating, as housings for poles.
He made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
He brought poles into rings on sides of altar, to carry it with them; hollow with boards he made it.
He made bronze basin and its bronze stand from mirrors of serving women who served at entrance of tent of meeting.
He made court: for south side, hangings of court of fine twisted linen, one hundred cubits.
Their twenty pillars and twenty bases were bronze; hooks of pillars and their bands silver.
For north side, one hundred cubits, their twenty pillars and twenty bases bronze; hooks of pillars and bands silver.
For west side, hangings fifty cubits, their ten pillars and ten bases; hooks of pillars and their bands silver.
For east side, toward sunrise, fifty cubits.
Hangings for one shoulder were fifteen cubits, their three pillars and three bases.
For second shoulder on this and that of gate of court, hangings fifteen cubits, their three pillars and three bases.
All hangings of court around were fine twisted linen.
Bases for pillars were bronze; hooks of pillars and their bands silver; overlay of their tops silver, and all pillars of court were banded with silver.
Screen of gate of court was work of embroiderer, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen; twenty cubits length and height in width five cubits, corresponding to hangings of court.
Their four pillars and four bases were bronze, their hooks silver, overlay of their tops and bands silver.
All pegs of dwelling-place and court around were bronze.
These are counted things of dwelling-place, dwelling-place of testimony, which were counted by mouth of Moses, service of Levites by hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of tribe of Judah, made all that YHWH commanded Moses.
With him Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of tribe of Dan, engraver, designer, and embroiderer in blue, purple, scarlet, and fine linen.
All gold used for work, in all work of holy place, gold of wave offering, was twenty-nine talents and seven hundred thirty shekels, by sanctuary shekel.
Silver of counted ones of congregation was one hundred talents and one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels, by sanctuary shekel.
A beka per head, half shekel by sanctuary shekel, for everyone passing over to counted ones from twenty years and upward, for six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty.
One hundred talents of silver were for casting bases of holy place and bases of veil; one hundred bases for one hundred talents, a talent per base.
From one thousand seven hundred seventy-five he made hooks for pillars, overlaid their tops, and banded them.
Bronze of wave offering was seventy talents and two thousand four hundred shekels.
With it he made bases for entrance of tent of meeting, bronze altar, bronze grating for it, and all vessels of altar,
bases of court around, bases of gate of court, all pegs of dwelling-place, and all pegs of court around.

translation_notes: Bronze basin is made from mirrors of women serving at tent entrance; preserve women's contribution and service.
Inventory by Ithamar emphasizes accountability for sacred materials.
Census silver becomes structural bases; equal half-shekel life-value supports the holy place.
safety_note: Sacred materials are counted transparently. This resists spiritualized financial opacity and honors every contribution, including women’s mirrors transformed into washing basin.
